title: Bookbot Outcomes, Repository Baseline, and Evidence · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved Bookbot run contains one plausible path, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, reporting, CLI, testing, performance, packaging, security, or release defect; identify the first failed boundary before changing code. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in Bookbot outcome, corpus, repository, and evidence charter and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Define the report user, decision, input corpus, output contract, consequence of error, and observable success before coding.
Record text owner, license or permission, source, version, checksum, language, and allowed use before analysis.
Inspect Git status, branch, remotes, configuration origin, tracked files, ignore rules, and baseline tests before editing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug User Decision
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Bookbot team handles case 3859 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the Bookbot outcome on user need, corpus authority, repository state, and changed-case evidence; keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define the report user, decision, input corpus, output contract, consequence of error, and observable success before coding.

Inspect the User Decision trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Evidence Ladder
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-evidence-ladder-1xn6xx" that uses case 6832 to gate the Bookbot outcome on user need, corpus authority, repository state, and changed-case evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Evidence Ladder.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Evidence Ladder into observable performance evidence. For Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Evidence Ladder, keep Evidence Ladder connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-evidence-ladder-1xn6xx
# Competency: bookbot-evidence-ladder.
# Case 6832: a multilingual community archive intake.
# Operating constraint: keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def evidence_bookbot_evidence_ladder_1xn6xx(user: str = "archive-reader", authorized: bool = True, acceptance_cases: int = 6, repository_clean: bool = True, changed_case_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1xn6xx = "1xn6xx-xx6nx1-6832"
    assert evidence_variant_1xn6xx
    evidence_axes_1xn6xx = (
        "corpus1xn6xx",
        "policyxx6nx1",
        "revision6832",
        "command1xn6xx6832",
        "reportxx6nx16832",
        "test68321xn6xx",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1xn6xx)) == 6
    if not user.strip() or acceptance_cases < 1:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "corpus-authority"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not changed_case_kept:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "evidence-gap"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 1
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-charter"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
